corrections.text: The article attributes to JUnit Pioneer's documentation the direct quote: "all tests annotated with `@DefaultLocale`, `@ReadsDefaultLocale`, and `WritesDefaultLocale` are scheduled in a way that guarantees correctness under mutation of shared global state." This phrasing does not appear verbatim in the source. The Pioneer documentation (https://junit-pioneer.org/docs/default-locale-timezone/) states that the extensions are "prepared for that and tests annotated with them will never execute in parallel (thanks to resource locks) to guarantee correct test results." The substance is accurate but the quoted text is a paraphrase, not a verbatim reproduction.

The article places two paraphrases from Marc Philipp's January 2026 blog post inside quotation marks as if they were direct quotes. The article quotes: "unintended thread spawning when tested code also used `ForkJoinPool`" — the source says the pool "would suddenly spawn additional threads" when the tested code also uses ForkJoinPool, and does not use the phrase "unintended thread spawning". The article also quotes: "will be opt-in in JUnit 6.1 with potential default adoption in 6.2, pending user feedback" — the source says "The new implementation will be an opt-in feature in JUnit 6.1. If we receive promising feedback in 6.1, we will switch the default to the new implementation in 6.2." Both paraphrases are conceptually accurate but were incorrectly presented as verbatim quotations.
